title: Sample Preparation (Urea/trypsin digestion) for Proteomics - Mammalian Organisms
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"appv7","text":"This procedure is to be used for preparation of peptide samples from proteins from mammalian samples. The peptide samples will be used for mass spectrometry based proteomic analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fjl6e","text":"If the samples to be prepared are from human origin or may contain infectious organisms, this procedure must  be performed in conditions meeting the bio-safety laboratory (BSL) requirements at your facility and adapted to the type of samples. This usually requires the use of Biosafety Cabinets (BSC's) for the procedure. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Use the method of your choice to extract the proteins from the samples (e.g. MPLEx procedure, sonication, tissue homogenization, etc.). Ensure that a BCA has been performed and that you have calculated the sample volume.

Add powdered form of Urea to sample to a target concentration of 8 M (0.745 * Sample Volume = mg of Urea). Note that the sample volume will increase to approximately: Sample Volume + (0.550*Sample Volume)
Vortex sample for 5-10 sec on highest setting or gently rotate end-over end until Urea is completely dissolved.
Prepare a 500 mM solution of Dithiothreitol (add 100 μL to 7.7 mg DTT No-Weigh tube). Add an appropriate volume of the DTT solution to obtain a 5 mM concentration in the sample.
Incubate in a thermomixer (or equivalent dry bath) at 37ºC for 1 hour, at 600-800 rpm.

Weigh out sufficient amount of iodoacetamide to have a final concentration of 400 mM (74 mg per 1 mL Nanopure water). Add an appropriate volume of iodoacetamide to obtain a 40 mM concentration in the sample.
Incubate in a thermomixer (or equivalent dry bath) at 37ºC for 1 hour, in the dark, at 600-800 rpm.
Pre-activate trypsin by incubating for 10 minutes at 37ºC.
Dilute the sample 8-fold with 50 mM NH4HCO3 to reduce the salt concentration.
Add sufficient amount of a 1M solution of CaCl2 to obtain a sample concentration of 1 mM CaCl2.

Digest sample for 3 hours with Trypsin @ 37ºC at a concentration of 1 μg trypsin/50 μg protein. (Note: at this stage after a quick freeze of the resulting peptides in liquid nitrogen, it can be stored at –80ºC until needed for analysis.)

Choose an appropriate solid phase extraction (SPE) C18 column. The capacity of each column is usually 5% of the bed weight. Therefore, a 25 mg column can hold up to 1.25 mg of digested peptides. C18 SPE cleanups are good for samples that do not contain any detergents.
Rinse column with 2 mL acidified water (0.1% TFA)
Slowly put sample solution through the column (no faster than 1 mL/minute).
Wash column containing sample with 4 mL of 95:5 H 2 O:ACN, 0.1% TFA.
Allow column to go to dryness and blot “needles” below columns dry.
Put collection tubes under column, and while SPE tubes are closed off from vacuum add 1 mL of 80:20 ACN:H 2 O, 0.1% TFA.
Allow elution buffer to flow slowly through column until column is dry.
Remove sample from SPE vacuum chamber.
Perform a BCA assay on sample, following the supplier's instructions. (Note: at this stage after a quick freeze of the sample in liquid nitrogen, it can be stored at –80ºC until needed for analysis.)
Concentrate sample in speedvac (or equivalent) to a volume of ~50-100 μL.

Dilute the samples in ultrapure water to the concentration of 0.1 μg of peptide per μL (or to the concentration of your choice). (note : we typically run 5 μL at 0.1 μg/μL in the Thermo Orbitrap instruments)

Vial sample with an excess volume to ensure that the volume to be injected by the autosampler will be smaller than the volume contained in the vial (note : we typically vial between 12 and 50 μL 0.1 μg/μL of peptides to ensure one or multiple injections of 5 μL at 0.1 μg/μL in the Thermo Orbitrap instruments)
Freeze the samples at -20 ºC until the samples are placed in the LC-autosampler. (note: to reduce peptide degradation, the samples should be run in the 2 months following preparation)

Condition column with 3 mL of MeOH. (note : Columns can withstand going to dryness between steps as long as the dry period is extended for no longer than 30 seconds. However, the columns cannot be allowed to go dry between the Methanol and 0.1% TFA steps.)
creator: Geremy Clair
